title: Domstack manifest
description: A normalized, revisioned manifest of public files emitted by a domstack build.

$schema.description: Versioned URL of the JSON Schema that describes this manifest.

version.description: SHA-256 hex digest derived from the final cache-relevant manifest entries.

generatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp for when domstack generated this manifest.

entries.description: Sorted public output entries included in the manifest after excludes and filters are applied.

items.description: One public output emitted by domstack and included in the reconciled domstack manifest.

outputRelname.description: Destination-relative output path using POSIX separators, such as "index.html" or "chunks/js/chunk-ABC.js".

kind.description: Classifies the build pipeline step or artifact type that produced this output.

url.description: Public same-origin URL for the output, normalized with a leading slash.

revision.description: SHA-256 hex digest of the output file contents. Null is reserved for outputs without a content revision.

bytes.description: Output file size in bytes. Null is reserved for outputs whose size is unavailable.

sourceRelname.description: Source-relative path that produced this output when a direct source file is known.

entryPoint.description: esbuild entry point path for script, style, worker, and service-worker outputs when available.

pagePath.description: Source-relative page path associated with this output when the output belongs to a page.

pageUrl.description: Canonical public page URL associated with this output when the output belongs to a page.

templatePath.description: Source-relative template path associated with this output when the output was emitted by a template.

page.description: Page-specific metadata recorded for manifest entries whose kind is "page".

path.description: Source-relative page path used by domstack routing, without a leading slash.

url.description: Canonical public URL for the page, such as "/" or "/docs/".

vars.description: Selected page variables that can affect offline or precache policy.

precache.description: Application-defined page precache policy metadata. Domstack records this value but does not interpret it.

offline.description: Application-defined page offline availability metadata. Domstack records this value but does not interpret it.
